message:
fix(connection): clear direct-INSERT append hint on normal autocommit close

The prepared direct-INSERT path keeps a `prepared_direct_insert_
append_hint` (a retained leaf image used to skip a fresh page walk
on hot append loops). The hint is only valid for the lifetime of
the transaction it was learned in: a different transaction may
have rewritten or split the same leaf out from under it.

The retained-autocommit close path already clears the hint when it
flushes the boundary. The matching plain autocommit close path
(reached when `autocommit_retain_enabled = false` or when the
implicit txn parked no retained writer) was not clearing it, so a
direct-INSERT append hint learned inside the just-finished
implicit transaction would leak into the next prepared-statement
execution. The next execution would then either reuse a stale leaf
pointer or trip the duplicate-pointer assertion landed in the
recent btree fix.

Fix:
- In the autocommit-close branch that sits inside the larger
  retained-autocommit handler, call
  `clear_prepared_direct_insert_append_hint()` immediately before
  the `concurrent_txn.set(false)` reset so the hint is invalidated
  in lock-step with the transaction tearing down.
- In the tail autocommit-close path that runs when no retained
  writer was parked, do the same after `txn_metrics_mark_finished()`.
  The accompanying comment spells out why: this path is only
  reached by autocommit closes that did not park a retained writer,
  so any hint observable here was learned inside the implicit
  transaction that just committed/rolled back.

Add `test_prepared_direct_simple_insert_normal_autocommit_clears_
append_hint`:
- Disables `autocommit_retain_enabled` to force the plain
  autocommit close path.
- Uses a file-backed database so the run exercises real pager I/O,
  not the :memory: short-circuit.
- Prepares a direct INSERT, executes it, then asserts
  `prepared_direct_insert_append_hint.borrow().is_none()` and
  re-reads the row to prove the insert itself succeeded.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
